Badges

Design fully custom badges — icon, label, colors, and description — then assign them to any user below. Badges appear next to a user's name in the sidebar, in this admin panel, and next to their name on records they've made public.
Backup, Restore & About

Backup & Restore

Database export/import creates a literal, full-fidelity copy of your database(s) — every field of every record (including formatted messages, custom fields, and the dictionary) is preserved exactly, not simplified into plain text. Use "Export Database" to save just the database selected above as a single .json file you can move to another device; use "Export All Databases" to back up everything at once. "Import Database(s)" reads such a file back in — it always creates brand-new database(s) alongside whatever is already on this device, so nothing existing is ever overwritten.

Games Data (Encrypted)

Backs up progress for every mini-game — Terminal, Minesweeper, Crack-a-Code, Match 3, Yahtzee, Find It, Typing, and the RNG gacha — covering stats, achievements, leaderboards, Battle Pass progress, and admin-tuned settings, for every user on this device. Unlike the database backup above, this file is encrypted with a password you set (via your browser's built-in Web Crypto API, not just obfuscated) before it's downloaded, so it's safe to store or share the file itself — only someone with the exact password can read or restore it. There's no password recovery: if you lose it, the backup is unrecoverable. Importing overwrites any existing games data on this device with the same keys; it does not touch your database records, dictionary, or user accounts.

About

The data is stored locally, meaning there is no server involved or someone that will mess with your data, you can download all of your saved data below. If you're not the only one using this device, please delete your data after downloading all the records.

App

This app can be installed to your home screen for offline, full-screen use.
🛠 Tools
🎮 Games
🛡 Admin
Formatting help
Type raw HTML directly — e.g. <b>bold</b>, <i>italic</i>, <ul><li>item</li></ul>, <a href="https://...">link</a>, <pre><code>...</code></pre>. It's sanitized and rendered when the record is saved.



Dictionary / Keywords

Keep reference terms and definitions here for this database. Use Insert on any entry to drop it into the Message field above at your cursor while writing a record.

Saved Records

Spreadsheet

Saved spreadsheets live in this database alongside your records and dictionary — open, edit, or create as many as you like, and switch between them anytime. Type a formula starting with = — arithmetic (=A1+B2*2, =2^10), comparisons (=A1>10), text concatenation (=A1&" "&B1), and functions like SUM, AVERAGE, IF, AND/OR, VLOOKUP, INDEX/MATCH, SUMIF/COUNTIF, and common text functions (CONCATENATE, LEFT/RIGHT/MID, UPPER/LOWER, LEN). Excel import/export preserves formula text, but any Excel function not in this list will show #NAME? until edited.
A1
Click a cell, or click-and-drag (or shift+click) to select a range, then use the toolbar above to format it. Drag the small square at the bottom-right corner of a selection to autofill — numeric sequences continue the pattern, formulas adjust their relative references, and anything else repeats. Drag the thin edge on a column or row header to resize it.
Tip: hover any row or column header and use its × to delete that specific row/column.

Math Question Generator

Generates practice math questions entirely in-app — nothing is sent anywhere. Fractions, exponents, and square roots are formatted with plain HTML/CSS locally, so it works fully offline. The chosen difficulty — including a Custom min/max range — applies to every question type, with no hidden caps. Statistics topics (Mean, Median, Mode, Range, Quartile, Decile, Percentile) generate a random data set and ask you to compute the requested value; quartiles use the median-of-halves method and percentiles/deciles use the nearest-rank method, both common classroom conventions.

Topics

Simple Equations — Advanced

Settings

Library

A local catalog of standalone entries built entirely out of custom fields — use it for anything you want to define once and reuse: inventory items, spells, NPCs, recipes, whatever fits. Each entry lives in this database alongside your records. Use Add to Record to drop an entry's fields straight into the Editor's custom fields.


Kanban

Boards live in this database alongside your records — create as many as you like and switch between them anytime. Each board has its own fully custom list of columns (call them "To Do / Doing / Done", or anything you want) — add, rename, reorder, or delete columns freely, then drag cards between them (or use each card's "Move to" dropdown).

Drawings

Freehand sketches saved locally in this database, just like your spreadsheets — keep as many as you like and switch between them anytime. Drawing autosaves a moment after you stop.

File Manager

A browsable view of everything saved in this database, plus your own freestanding text/code files with a built-in editor. Tap any item below to jump to it, or use the editor to create or edit a file directly.

Everything in this database

Folders

Organize the files below into colored folders, nested as deep as you like (a folder can live inside another folder). Deleting a folder also deletes any subfolders inside it, but never deletes files — they just become uncategorized again.

Files

Plain-text/code files stored right in this database — notes, snippets, config, whatever you like.

Public Records

Records that anyone (on any account on this device) has chosen to mark public show up here, no matter which database or account they belong to. To publish one of your own records, check "Make this record public" while creating or editing it in the Editor tab; uncheck it and save again to remove it from this list.

Terminal

A little offline hacking-style minigame — explore a fake filesystem with ls, cd, and cat, find passwords hidden in files to unlock locked directories, and track down the flags. Clear every flag in a round and the mainframe regenerates a brand new, harder layout just for you — it never ends. Your round, total flags collected, score, and Battle Pass are saved to your account. Everything is simulated locally; no real files or network are touched. Type help to see every command.
Score: 0
Shop — spend your score
Upgrades are permanent and carry over every round. Prices go up each time you buy the same upgrade again.
Battle Pass
Every point you earn anywhere in the terminal — reading files, unlocking directories, capturing flags, clearing rounds — also earns that many Battle Pass XP. Leveling up pays out a one-time bonus of spendable score and a rank like "Recruit 1". Each tier (e.g. Recruit, Initiate, Operative...) spans several levels before you're promoted to the next one. It never caps out; once every tier's been used it starts over at a higher numeral (e.g. "Legend II 1").
Community Levels — build & play custom levels
Anyone can design their own fake filesystem — directories, files, locked vaults, flags — and publish it here for everyone else to explore. Playing (or building) a community level is purely for fun: it never touches your score, rank, or Battle Pass. Type newlevel <name> below or in the terminal to start building (then mkdir/touch/write/flag/lockdir/ motd to shape it and savelevel to publish), or just pick one from the list and jump in with playlevel <id>.
guest@mainframe:/$

Terminal Game — Admin

This is a never-ending game: once a player clears every flag in their current round, the mainframe procedurally generates a new, harder layout just for them, forever. There's a shared round 1 — the starting world every new player sees — and it's no longer edited as raw JSON. Instead, build it live in the terminal above using admin-only commands: editfs to enter edit mode, then mkdir, touch, write, rm, flag, lockdir, unlockdir and motd to shape it, savefs to publish it as round 1 for every new player, and stopedit to return to your own game. Type help in the terminal while logged in as admin to see the full list.

Scoring

Point values players earn for each action. These are plain numbers you set here — no filesystem JSON editing needed. Changes apply to players' next action.

Shop Pricing

Base cost and effect size for each shop upgrade. Actual cost rises each level a player buys (base × 1.6 per level already owned).

Difficulty Presets

Players choose one of these with the difficulty command; it controls their procedural rounds (round 2+). Vault/folder multiplier scales the normal round-based count, capped at the number given. Depth chances is a comma-separated list of percentages, one per extra directory level below each top-level folder — each level is only rolled if the previous one succeeded, so more/higher numbers mean deeper filesystems. 2nd password chance is the odds a vault gets wrapped in an extra "checkpoint" lock that needs its own separate password before the vault's own password is even reachable. Points/XP bonus adds a flat extra % on top of every point (and, for XP bonus, every Battle Pass XP award) earned while playing on that difficulty -- stacks additively with the shop's Score Multiplier and XP Rush upgrades.
Easy
Normal
Hard
Nightmare

Battle Pass

Every point a player earns anywhere in the game also earns that much Battle Pass XP. Set how much XP level 1 costs and, optionally, a % by which the XP cost grows every level after that (0% keeps every level the same cost; e.g. 10% means level 2 costs 10% more XP than level 1, level 3 costs 10% more than level 2, and so on) — plus the one-time score bonus paid out per level-up (base + this much extra per level). Tiers are awarded in order, one per line below — once the list runs out it repeats with a higher numeral tier (e.g. "Legend II 1").
Each line is a tier name. Add :N to make a tier span N levels, shown as "Recruit 1", "Recruit 2" ... "Recruit N" before moving to the next tier — e.g. Recruit:3 gives 3 Recruit levels. Leave off :N for a single-level tier. Edit the number to change how many levels any tier lasts.

Ranks

Players are promoted automatically as their cumulative score crosses each threshold below, with a promotion banner printed in their terminal. The rank with the lowest threshold is everyone's starting rank, so keep at least one rank at 0 points.

Player Progress

Typing Test

Type the passage below as fast and accurately as you can. Timing starts on your first keystroke and stops the moment you finish. This has its own separate score and leaderboard — completely unrelated to the Terminal game's score, rank, or Battle Pass.
Your best: –
WPM: 0 Accuracy: 100% Time: 0.0s
Leaderboard
Best run per player, ranked by WPM (ties broken by accuracy). Only finished passages with at least 80% accuracy are saved.

Generator

Generates UUIDs, hexadecimal strings, random numbers, and passphrases entirely in-app using your browser's cryptographic random number generator — nothing is sent anywhere.
UUID

Settings

Hexadecimal

Settings

Number

Settings

Passphrase
Words are picked from the EFF's Short Wordlist #1 (1,296 memorable words, ~10.3 bits of entropy each) using the same cryptographic randomness as the rest of this tab — a longer passphrase of ordinary words is both stronger and easier to type/remember than a short string of random characters.

Settings

Text & Data Toolkit

A grab bag of everyday text/data utilities — counting, case conversion, Base64/URL encoding, JSON formatting, hashing, and a line-by-line diff. Everything runs entirely in your browser; nothing is sent anywhere.

Match 3

Swap adjacent tiles to line up 3 or more of the same kind. Chain reactions score bonus points. This has its own separate score, stats, achievements, and Battle Pass — completely unrelated to the Terminal game's score/rank/Battle Pass or the Find It / Minesweeper / Typing test scores.
Score: 0 Your best: –
Objectives
An endless run of procedurally-generated levels — each one asks you to collect specific fruits within a limited number of moves, and levels keep getting tougher the higher you climb, with no final level. Pick a difficulty — it scales the targets and move limit up or down — and your progress is tracked separately per difficulty. Playing a level uses the same board above; it doesn't affect your endless score/leaderboard run.
Battle Pass
Every banked run earns Battle Pass XP equal to its score. Leveling up pays out a one-time bonus of Match 3 Points and a title. This Battle Pass is entirely separate from the Terminal / Minesweeper / Find It Battle Passes.
Achievements
Leaderboard
Best completed run per player. Click "New Game" to bank your current score and start fresh.

Match 3 — Admin

Configure the Match 3 Battle Pass (its own XP curve and titles, separate from the Terminal, Minesweeper, and Find It games') and manage individual players' XP or stats.

Battle Pass

Set how much XP level 1 costs and, optionally, a % by which the XP cost grows every level after that (0% keeps every level the same cost) — plus the one-time Match 3 Points bonus paid out per level-up (base + this much extra per level).
Same format as the other Battle Passes: add :N to make a tier span N levels (e.g. Matcher:3 gives 3 Matcher levels). Leave off :N for a single-level tier.

Objectives

Control how many different fruit types a level can ask players to collect at once. Raising the cap adds more simultaneous objectives to later levels; the "bonus moves" field adds moves back per extra objective so those levels stay winnable instead of just harder.

Find It

A little Progressbar95-style spot-the-pattern puzzle: a target string is shown below, scattered among a grid of similar-looking decoys. Click every occurrence of the target before the timer runs out to advance to a harder level. Wrong clicks cost you time. This has its own separate score, leaderboard, and Battle Pass — completely unrelated to the Terminal game's score/rank/Battle Pass or the Match 3 / Minesweeper / Typing test scores.

Every banked run earns Battle Pass XP equal to its score. Leveling up pays out a one-time bonus of Find It Points and a title. This Battle Pass is entirely separate from the Terminal / Minesweeper Battle Passes.

Minesweeper

Classic Minesweeper. Left-click a cell to reveal it; right-click (or long-press on mobile) to flag a suspected mine — or turn on Flag Mode below to flag with a plain tap instead, no holding required. Clear every safe cell without detonating one to win. This has its own separate stats and leaderboard, and its own separate Battle Pass — completely unrelated to the Terminal game's score/rank/Battle Pass or the Match 3 / Find It / Typing scores.
Mines: 10 Time: 0s 🪙 0 Your best: –
Go past Expert's 30×16/99 mines for bigger XP, Battle Pass, and Mine Coin payouts — rewards scale up with board size and mine density beyond Expert's parameters.
Battle Pass
Every win earns Battle Pass XP — more for harder difficulties and faster times. Custom boards scale XP by size and mine density, so going past Expert's 30×16/99 parameters earns even more than Expert itself. Leveling up pays out a one-time bonus of Minesweeper Points and a title. This Battle Pass is entirely separate from the Terminal game's.
Item Shop
Opening a cell has a small chance of turning up Mine Coins — occasionally you'll hit a jackpot. Spend coins here on consumables you can use mid-game, a permanent coin-luck upgrade, or cosmetic board skins.
Leaderboard
Fastest win per player, per difficulty. Custom entries also show the board size/mine count used, since times aren't directly comparable across different Custom configs.

Minesweeper — Admin

Configure the Minesweeper Battle Pass (its own XP curve and titles, separate from the Terminal game's) and manage individual players' XP or stats.

Battle Pass

Set how much XP level 1 costs and, optionally, a % by which the XP cost grows every level after that (0% keeps every level the same cost) — plus the one-time Minesweeper Points bonus paid out per level-up (base + this much extra per level).
Same format as the Terminal Battle Pass: add :N to make a tier span N levels (e.g. Sweeper:3 gives 3 Sweeper levels). Leave off :N for a single-level tier.

Player Progress

Crack-a-Code

Classic code-breaking: a secret sequence of colored pegs is hidden (repeats allowed). Build a guess from the palette below and submit it — each guess gets scored with black pegs (right color, right spot) and white pegs (right color, wrong spot). Crack it within the guess limit to win. Supports up to 12 colors, with adjustable code length and palette size for Custom games, plus a shared once-a-day Daily Puzzle. This has its own separate stats and Battle Pass — unrelated to every other game's.
Longer codes and more colors are exponentially harder — and earn more Battle Pass XP.
Tap a color to fill the next open peg in your current guess. Tap a filled peg to clear it and everything after it.
Battle Pass
Every solved code earns Battle Pass XP — more for longer codes, more colors, and fewer guesses used. The Daily Puzzle pays out extra XP, plus a streak bonus for consecutive days solved. Leveling up pays out a one-time bonus of Crack-a-Code Points and a title. This Battle Pass is entirely separate from every other game's.
Today's Daily Puzzle Leaderboard
Fewest guesses to solve today's puzzle, one entry per player.

Crack-a-Code — Admin

Configure the Crack-a-Code Battle Pass (its own XP curve and titles, separate from every other game's) and manage individual players' XP or stats.

Battle Pass

Set how much XP level 1 costs and, optionally, a % by which the XP cost grows every level after that (0% keeps every level the same cost) — plus the one-time Crack-a-Code Points bonus paid out per level-up (base + this much extra per level).
Same format as the other games' Battle Passes: add :N to make a tier span N levels (e.g. Codebreaker:3 gives 3 Codebreaker levels). Leave off :N for a single-level tier.

Daily Puzzle

Length / colors / max guesses used for every player's Daily Puzzle (it's the same code for everyone, each day).

Player Progress

Yahtzee

Classic dice game: each of the 13 rounds gives you up to 3 rolls of 5 dice (tap a die between rolls to hold it), then you lock the roll into one of the 13 scoring categories below. Fill the upper section (Ones–Sixes) to 63+ for a 35-point bonus. Play unlimited Custom Games, or the shared once-a-day Daily Challenge where every die roll is the same for everyone. This has its own separate stats and Battle Pass — unrelated to every other game's.
Battle Pass
Every finished game earns Battle Pass XP equal to its final score. The Daily Challenge pays out extra XP, plus a streak bonus for consecutive days played. Leveling up pays out a one-time bonus of Yahtzee Points and a title. This Battle Pass is entirely separate from every other game's.
All-Time Leaderboard
Highest final game score, one entry per player.
Today's Daily Challenge Leaderboard
Highest score on today's shared Daily Challenge, one entry per player.

Yahtzee — Admin

Configure the Yahtzee Battle Pass (its own XP curve and titles, separate from every other game's), the Daily Challenge XP bonus, and manage individual players' XP or stats.

Set how much XP level 1 costs and, optionally, a % by which the XP cost grows every level after that (0% keeps every level the same cost) — plus the one-time Yahtzee Points bonus paid out per level-up (base + this much extra per level).
Same format as the other games' Battle Passes: add :N to make a tier span N levels (e.g. Roller:3 gives 3 Roller levels). Leave off :N for a single-level tier.

Daily Challenge

Extra Battle Pass XP earned, as a % of normal, for finishing the Daily Challenge (on top of the streak bonus).

Sudoku

Fill the 9×9 grid so every row, column, and 3×3 box contains 1–9 exactly once. Pick a difficulty, tap a cell, then tap a number below (or use your keyboard). Every puzzle you finish earns Battle Pass XP — harder difficulties and flawless solves (no wrong entries) pay out more. This has its own separate stats and Battle Pass, unrelated to every other game's.

Every solved puzzle earns Battle Pass XP based on difficulty, with a bonus for solving without mistakes. Leveling up unlocks a claimable Sudoku Points reward.

Once every tier's been used it repeats with a higher numeral (e.g. "Sudoku Grandmaster II 1").

Add Custom Tier

Add your own rarity tier — give it a name, an icon/emoji, a color, its odds, and how many points it's worth. It'll show up in the odds list, the roll pool, and everyone's collection right away.

Player Progress

Face Card Never Decides

Everyone draws 5 cards and starts with 3 lives. Each round, everyone plays and reveals one card at once. You win the round by playing the highest-numbered card, an Ace, or a boosted numeric card — Aces are always the highest plain-number card in the game. A face card (J/Q/K) never competes or wins by rank on its own; instead, playing one requires you to pair it with a second card from your hand, and the face card's suit triggers an ability that’s applied to that paired card. Lose all 3 lives and you're eliminated. Last player standing wins the game.
Power-Up Draft
Before hands are dealt, everyone drafts one covered power-up card, one player at a time. You can't see what's underneath until every player has picked — then all picks are revealed at once. Your power-up sticks with you for the rest of the run.
🔴 Red Boost — +3 (−3 while Joker rules are active) to your numbered cards (2–10 only), for the whole run.
⚡ Zap — your face card abilities are 50% more effective. 5 uses for the whole run.
‼️ Second Wind — the first time you'd be eliminated, you're saved with 2 hearts instead. One-time, automatic.
🟨 Execution — force any other player to lose a life this round, no matter what they play. 2 uses for the whole run.
🐟 Big Hand — start with 10 cards instead of 5, but your played card values are reduced 20%, for the whole run.
🔀 Reshuffle — return cards from your hand and redraw that many. 1 use per round, 5 uses per run.
🎮 Manipulate — swap a card in your hand for any card you pick from the remaining deck, at the cost of 1 life. One-time use.
📶 Signal Boost — apply any face card suit ability to yourself without needing to play a face card. 2 uses for the whole run.
🖤 Black Boost — +3 (−3 while Joker rules are active) to the paired card of any face card you play, for the whole run.
🍀 Lucky Seven — any plain 7 you play is worth 5 more, for the whole run.
🛡️ Iron Skin — 50% chance to not lose a life whenever you lose a round, for the whole run.
🔥 Phoenix — the first time you'd be eliminated, you're saved with 1 heart instead. One-time, automatic.
👻 Ghost — immune to being targeted by an opponent's Execution, for the whole run.
🎲 High Roller — +4 to any Ace you play, for the whole run.
🎯 Double Down — whenever you win a round outright (not tied), draw 1 extra card, for the whole run.
⚖️ Warden — whenever you play a Joker, also gain 1 life, for the whole run.
🪩 Golden Touch — +1 to every card you play, no matter its rank, for the whole run.
🐾 Underdog — +5 to your card this round whenever you're tied for the fewest lives among players still in the game, for the whole run.
🎰 Gambler — 25% chance your card's value is doubled this round, for the whole run.
🎒 Hoarder — start with 7 cards instead of 5, no downside, for the whole run.
🎗️ Near Miss — if you lose a round by less than 3 value, you don't lose a life, for the whole run.
🗃️ Collector — draw 1 extra card whenever you win a round, even a tied one, for the whole run.
🃏 Jester — your Jokers are worth 20 instead of 14, unbeatable by any Ace, for the whole run.
💪 Comeback — the first time you fall to exactly 1 life, permanently gain +3 to all future plays for the rest of the run. One-time, automatic.
Suit Abilities (face cards only)
Playing a face card (Joker excepted) is a two-card play: select the face card, then select a second card from your hand to pair with it. The face card itself never competes; the paired card is what competes this round on your behalf, and the face card's suit determines how it's affected.
♠ Spades — you draw 2 cards. The paired card competes at its plain value.
♦ Diamonds — disables every other player's Ace card(s) this round. The paired card competes at its plain value.
♣ Clubs — reserves 50% of the paired card's own value; that reserved amount is added onto your next competing card (whatever you play next), instead of this one. Also exempts you from losing a life this round. Playing another Club before the reserve is used replaces it.
♥ Hearts — increases the paired card's value by 50% of its own value, applied immediately.
If a face card is the only card left in your hand, it's played alone with no pair — its ability doesn't trigger and it doesn't compete, so it plays like a dead card for the round. Also: if your hand ever drops to 1 card, you immediately draw 2 more. If your hand ever grows past 5 cards, you discard down to 5 after the round.
Special Cards
Ace — the highest plain-number card in the game (a boosted paired card can still beat it).
Joker — acts as an Ace (still the highest card), is played alone with no pairing, but flips the rules of the game: instead of the highest card winning, the lowest numbered card wins, and every suit ability reverses. Playing a Joker never costs you a life, win or lose:
♠ Spades — discards 2 cards instead of drawing.
♦ Diamonds — enables everyone else's Aces this round instead, and disables its own player's Ace.
♣ Clubs — the reserved value is subtracted from your next competing card instead of added.
♥ Hearts — decreases the paired card's value by 50% instead of increasing it.
Playing another Joker flips the rules back to normal.

Power-Up Draft — pick a covered card

Earn XP by playing Face Card Never Decides — same endless Battle Pass design as the Terminal minigame. XP required per level scales up the further you go, so it never caps out; leveling up raises your rank (e.g. "Newcomer 1"). Certain levels also unlock card back themes and titles you can equip below. Separate from Deck of Spies' own Battle Pass.

Face Card Never Decides — Admin

Set how many lives (hearts) every player — including bots — starts a new game with. Takes effect the next time a game is started; it doesn't change lives in a game that's already in progress.

Battle Pass

Tune the endless XP curve and rank titles — same system as the Terminal minigame's Battle Pass.
Add extra reward levels (titles or card back themes) for Face Card Never Decides. New rewards are appended right after the highest existing level. Custom rewards are stored locally on this device/browser, same as the rest of the Battle Pass.

Deck of Spies

A solitaire deck-builder. Infiltrate spy networks by ending the game with more Key Spies (J, Q, K) in your own deck than the enemy holds in the Spy Discard pile. Each turn, deal with the Spy Card on top of the Spy Deck: pay it off, turn it to your side, or sacrifice a Double Agent to make it go away.
Setup
Draw Deck: all Aces, 2s, 3s and 4s (16 cards). Aces are Double Agents — wild for Recruiting and the only cards you can sacrifice to Assassinate.
Spy Deck: cards 5–K, stacked so 5s/6s/7s come up first, then 8s/9s/10s, then the Jacks/Queens/Kings (each tier shuffled).
You start with 5 cards in hand.
Actions (pick one each turn)
1. Counter — discard hand cards (any suits) with total value ≥ the Spy Card's value. Values: A=1, 2–10=face, J=11, Q=12, K=13. The Spy Card goes to the Spy Discard — you don't keep it.
2. Recruit — discard cards matching the Spy Card's suit (highlighted) with total value ≥ the Spy Card. The Spy Card joins your own deck — you keep it! An Ace (Double Agent) matches any suit.
3. Assassinate — if you can't or won't Counter or Recruit, sacrifice an Ace. The Ace is removed from the game, the Spy Card goes to the Spy Discard, your entire hand is discarded, and your turn ends immediately. Run out of Aces and you lose!
Optional: Retire
After a Counter or Recruit, you may discard 2 or more cards of the same value from your hand to remove them from your deck permanently — useful for thinning out weak low cards (but be careful not to retire away Key Spies you've recruited!).
Game End & Scoring
When the Spy Deck is empty, count Key Spies (J, Q, K). Score = (Your Key Spies) − (Enemy Key Spies in the Spy Discard). You win if your score is positive.

🏆 Battle Pass
Earn XP by playing Deck of Spies — same endless Battle Pass design as the Terminal minigame. XP required per level scales up the further you go, so it never caps out; leveling up raises your rank (e.g. "Trainee 1"). Certain levels also unlock card back themes and titles you can equip below. Separate from Face Card Never Decides' own Battle Pass.

Equip

Card Back
Title

Rewards

Deck of Spies — Admin

Battle Pass

Tune the endless XP curve and rank titles — same system as the Terminal minigame's Battle Pass.
Add extra reward levels (titles or card back themes) for Deck of Spies. New rewards are appended right after the highest existing level. Custom rewards are stored locally on this device/browser, same as the rest of the Battle Pass.

♦ Set

Based on the real-life card game Set: every card has 4 features (number, color, shape, shading), and a valid "Set" is any 3 cards where, feature by feature, the values are either all the same or all different across the three. Tap 3 cards to check them. Play unlimited Custom Games, or the shared once-a-day Daily Puzzle (same deal for every player) where the goal is to find every Set hidden in the grid. This has its own separate stats and Battle Pass — unrelated to every other game's.
Cards left in deck: 0
Battle Pass
Every Set found earns Battle Pass XP. The Daily Puzzle pays out extra XP, plus a streak bonus for consecutive days solved. Leveling up pays out a one-time bonus of Set Points and a title. This Battle Pass is entirely separate from every other game's.
Today's Daily Puzzle Leaderboard
Fewest mistakes (then fastest time) to find every Set in today's puzzle, one entry per player.

Set — Admin

Configure the Set Battle Pass (its own XP curve and titles, separate from every other game's), the Daily Puzzle's card count, and manage individual players' XP or stats.

Battle Pass

Set how much XP level 1 costs and, optionally, a % by which the XP cost grows every level after that (0% keeps every level the same cost) — plus the one-time Set Points bonus paid out per level-up (base + this much extra per level).
Same format as the other games' Battle Passes: add :N to make a tier span N levels (e.g. Spotter:3 gives 3 Spotter levels). Leave off :N for a single-level tier.

Daily Puzzle

How many cards are dealt for every player's Daily Puzzle (it's the same deal for everyone, each day). Must be a multiple of 3.

Player Progress

📦 Case Opening

Open cases for a chance at Common through Legendary trophies. Case Opening has its own Battle Pass below (mirrors the Terminal game's Battle Pass, same admin-configurable XP curve and titles, but entirely separate progress), separate from the Face Cards / Deck of Spies Battle Pass. Earn Keys by playing Face Card Never Decides and Deck of Spies, or claim a free one on cooldown.

🏆 Battle Pass

A progression track just for Case Opening, mirroring the Terminal game's Battle Pass (same XP-per-level curve and title format) but entirely separate from it and from the Face Cards / Deck of Spies Battle Pass. Every case opened earns Battle Pass XP; leveling up pays out a one-time bonus of Keys plus a title, automatically.

🎰 Roulette

Standard European roulette (single 0, numbers 1–36). Place a bet, spin, and watch the same reel-style reveal as Case Opening land on your number. Every new player starts with a 100 Money headstart. Roulette has its own Battle Pass below, mirroring the Terminal game's Battle Pass (same admin-configurable XP curve and title format) but entirely separate from every other game's.
💰 0 Money Out of Money — reset to play again.

🏆 Battle Pass

A progression track just for Roulette, mirroring the Terminal game's Battle Pass (same XP-per-level curve and title format) but entirely separate from it and from every other game's Battle Pass. Every spin earns Battle Pass XP (more for a win); leveling up pays out a one-time bonus of Money plus a title, automatically.

🦊 Zebra Puzzle

A grid deduction puzzle in the style of the classic "Einstein's Riddle". Clues refer to items by symbol (see the Legend to decode them) -- use them to work out which item belongs to which house, then fill in the grid below. Zebra Puzzle has its own Battle Pass below, mirroring Roulette's Battle Pass (same admin-configurable XP curve and title format) but entirely separate from every other game's.

    🏆 Battle Pass

    A progression track just for Zebra Puzzle, mirroring Roulette's Battle Pass (same XP-per-level curve and title format) but entirely separate from it and from every other game's Battle Pass. Every solved puzzle earns Battle Pass XP (more for solving it cleanly); leveling up pays out a one-time bonus of Insight plus a title, automatically.

    🏭 Factory

    A simple idle game: click to assemble Widgets by hand, or buy generators that each churn out Widgets on their own, every second, for as long as this app is open in a tab — even one you're not looking at. Each generator costs more the more of it you own. Progress is per-account, and it catches up on what you earned while the app itself was closed (capped at a few hours) the next time you sign in.
    🧵 0 Widgets

    Generators

    Upgrades

    ⚫ Santri

    Rock-Paper-Scissors meets chess. Circle beats Slash, Slash beats Square, Square beats Circle. 🟠 Square moves like a rook (straight lines), 🔴 Circle moves like a bishop (diagonals), and ╱ Slash jumps like a knight. Land a piece on the gold center tile to transform it to the next type in the cycle. Instead of moving, you may place a barrier: tap an empty non-home square, then tap one of the highlighted sides to wall it off. A wall only stops straight-line movement (Square) crossing that side — Circle slides diagonally through the square's corners, and Slash jumps clear over it, so neither is blocked by a wall. In 2 Player, capture every enemy piece to win. In 4 Player it's free-for-all on a 7×7 board against Soren, Atlas, and Riven — be the last one with pieces left standing. Play against the computer — there's no online matchmaking here.

    Legend

    Circle — moves diagonally, any distance — beats Slash
    Slash — jumps like a knight — beats Square
    Square — moves straight, any distance — beats Circle
    Walls are colored by whoever placed them, and flash briefly when placed
    Record: 0W – 0L
